summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an all‑magic 1989 show, highlighted by killer Looks Like Rain, a super Terrapin Station and soaring Crazy Fingers.
review: The recording delivers crystal‑clear soundboard quality (aside from a left/right channel swap) that lets listeners hear every nuance of the band’s magical performance. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s soaring lead on "Looks Like Rain", a bubbling "Terrapin" and a transcendent "Crazy Fingers", while the first‑set trio of "Row Jimmy", "Masterpiece" and "Built To Last" is praised as perfect bedtime listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (mostly 5‑star) and uniformly glowing comments about both the musical performance and the sound quality indicate an outstanding show with excellent recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix throughout; only issue noted is left/right channel reversal causing Brent’s organ to be heard from the opposite speaker.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the concert as a seamless, unhurried journey that feels like "smooth as a rhapsody," with magical moments building from the first set into an expansive second‑set groove.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead on "Looks Like Rain" is called “killer” and given new meaning by his tone.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s B3 organ is likened to an octopus playing an underwater pipe‑organ, a highlight of the night.
